title: Use web-discovery to enrich and verify source coverage
scope: Perform a focused source-discovery and source-retrieval enrichment pass using the web-discovery skill and ketch. Treat T010-T040 as first-pass research because those workers may have launched before web-discovery was installed.
goals: Explicitly use the web-discovery workflow: prefer ketch search/docs/code for discovery and ketch scrape for direct retrieval; use curl only as fallback when appropriate.
Check whether the first-pass handoffs missed important primary sources, project pages, marketplace listings, docs pages, GitHub repositories, or plugin pages.
Add or correct source-backed facts that materially affect recommendations for VS Code, Obsidian, subscriptions, or open-source/self-hosted architectures.
Flag any page that requires browser rendering/session access as needs-review instead of treating it as no evidence.
Produce a concise enrichment handoff that synthesis must consume.
non_goals: Do not rewrite the four first-pass handoffs.
Do not write the final report.
Do not perform unlimited broad search; focus on material gaps and high-risk claims.
safety: Do not read tmp/, archive/, or prompt-buffer.md.
Do not edit files tagged human-written.
Do not use browser automation or sessionful retrieval.
Keep output as valid JSON using autocomplete-research-handoff-v1 where possible.
success_criteria: Record whether ketch was available and which web-discovery routes were used.
List material new sources, corrections, coverage gaps, and recommended synthesis changes.
Every correction or new nontrivial fact has source URLs, evidence type, last_checked, and confidence.
